title: Immediate Run Test Execution Guide
content: This immediate-run test validates that a backend job or endpoint can be triggered on demand and completes correctly under production-like constraints. Objective: confirm manual invocation path, execution correctness, observability, and safe failure handling without waiting for scheduled windows. Preconditions: test environment is healthy, required secrets/config are loaded, target dependencies are reachable, and a known input dataset is prepared with expected outcomes. Trigger validation: execute the run-now action via approved API or control plane, verify request authentication/authorization, and confirm the run is uniquely identified with a traceable run ID. Runtime checks: ensure the job transitions through expected states (queued, running, completed/failed), starts within acceptable delay, and respects configured concurrency and timeout limits. Functional checks: verify business logic outputs, database mutations, emitted events, and side effects exactly match acceptance rules; ensure idempotency if the same trigger is retried. Safety checks: confirm guardrails prevent duplicate overlapping runs when disallowed, enforce rate limits, and block destructive operations outside allowed scope. Failure-path checks: inject controlled errors (dependency timeout, invalid payload, transient network issue), verify retries/backoff behavior, and ensure terminal failures produce actionable diagnostics without partial corruption. Observability requirements: capture structured logs, metrics, and traces linked by run ID; include start/end timestamps, duration, result summary, and error classification. Post-run validation: reconcile output counts, verify data integrity constraints, and confirm no orphaned locks, stale queue items, or leaked temporary artifacts remain. Reporting template: include trigger method, input version, environment, run ID, execution timeline, pass/fail by check, detected risks, and remediation owner. Exit criteria: successful on-demand invocation, deterministic and correct results, complete telemetry, and documented rollback/mitigation steps for any non-passing scenario.
